name: French Herb Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine 2 cups flour, yeast, and 3 teaspoons dry salad dressing mix. Reserve the remaining mix.
2. Heat buttermilk, water, and shortening until warm (120-130°F), ensuring the shortening is not fully melted.
3. Add the heated mixture to the flour mixture. Incorporate the egg. Blend until moistened; beat for 3 minutes at medium speed.
4. Gradually add enough flour to form a firm dough. Knead on a well-floured surface until smooth and elastic (5-10 minutes).
5. Place the dough in a greased bowl and turn to grease the top. Cover; let rise in a warm oven (turn the oven on its lowest setting for 1 minute, then turn it off) for 20 minutes.
6. Punch down the dough. Divide into 2 parts. On a lightly floured surface, roll or pat each part to a 12x7 inch rectangle.
7. Starting with the longer side, roll up tightly, sealing edges and ends. Place seam-side down on a greased cookie sheet.
8. Make diagonal slashes about 2 inches apart in the tops of the loaves. Cover; let rise in a warm oven until light and doubled (about 30 minutes).
9. Preheat the oven to 375°F. Bake for 25-30 minutes, brushing with melted butter while warm and sprinkling with 1 teaspoon of the reserved salad dressing mix.
10. Cool on wire racks.
